title: 11 Settlement Procedures
body_text:
11	Settlement Procedures
11.1	Settlement for Interac
Â®
e-Transfer Transactions
Refer to the Interac e-Transfer
Â®
Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports Part for settlement information on e-Transfer transactions.
11.2	Settlement for Wire Transactions
The Wires application isnât connected to your banking system. Cancelled wires arenât automatically credited back to your customer. Follow your internal procedures for managing cancelled wires including settlement to the customerâs account. See the Wires Settlement Chapter in the Wires Part for more information.
11.3	EFM Fraud Report
Central 1 provides an EFM Fraud Report that includes Key Performance Indicators (KPI) standard to financial institutions. The report is available in your financial institutionâs root folder on Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
Description
The EFM Fraud Report provides KPI data and a detailed list of alerted transactions, sorted by transaction date, along with the yearâs totals.
Purpose
Use this report to identify transactions classified as risk and to see your decisioning on the legitimacy of the transactions.
Organization
The report is in Excel format and contains two pages. The Aggregated Overview Report provides data on Operations, Analytics, and Losses for the month. The Alerted Transaction Report lists alerted transactions on EFM and your decisioning on the legitimacy of the transactions. See Figure 1 and Figure 2.
Distribution
The report is available on the 15th of the month (or on the following business day) and is distributed to all participating financial institutions.
